The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: Washington Post: 29 May, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia squeezes Luhansk in ‘indescribably difficult’ battles, Zelensky says
  • Colombians vote for president in tense, potentially historic election
  • U.N. human rights chief disappoints Uyghur advocates on visit to China
  • Ukraine war volunteers are coming home, reckoning with difficult fight
  • Russia claims capture of key Ukrainian transport hub
  • Agatha forecast to hit Mexico as Category 2 hurricane and enter Gulf

  • First TS forms off Mexico's coast, to become hurricane

  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 29 May, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 29 May, 2022 published at 2022-05-29 10:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Public appeal for yearly review of Hong Kong’s minimum wage dismissed as ‘unfeasible’ by city’s labour minister

  • Coronavirus in China: Covid tests for the dead spark online mockery in Shenzhen

  • Philippines President-elect Marcos Jnr chooses stability in naming new cabinet to revive economy and restore family name

  • Coronavirus: North Korea’s Kim Jong-un says pandemic situation controlled and improving
  • Russian oil and Indian money for suffering Sri Lanka

  • Gustavo Petro, the ex-rebel front runner in Colombian vote, could shake US ties
  • ‘Now I am a beggar’: fleeing the Russian advance in Ukraine
  • Online Instagram threats by Uvalde shooter went unreported, dismissed as harmless ‘kids joke around like that’
  • Ukraine war: Russia test-fires latest hypersonic Zircon missile with a range of 1,000 kms
  • Real Madrid beat Liverpool 1-0 in Champions League final delayed amid chaos over ‘fake tickets’
  • Stampede at Nigeria church charity fair leaves 31 dead and seven injured
  • South Korean actor and director shine at Cannes Film Festival
  • Thousands of protesters in Madrid call for ban on prostitution in Spain
  • McDonald’s in Russia could be called ‘Fun and Tasty,’ after ‘McDuck’ is ruled out
  • Death toll reaches 29 after shopping centre under construction collapses in Iran
  • Judge approves US$1 billion settlement for families of victims in Florida building collapse
  • Investigators question delayed police response in Texas school shooting
  • Ukraine war: Russia takes small cities, aims to seize all of the contested Donbas region
  • Cardinal Sodano, controversial right-hand man to two popes and Vatican power broker for decades, dies at 94
  • Climate change: The world isn’t spending enough to prevent disasters, UN says
  • Monkeypox: Don’t make the same mistake as you did with coronavirus, disease experts urge WHO and governments
  • How Bangladesh has surged past India on road to South Asian economic stardom
  • Start-up founders joining Finnish relocation programme want to grow Hong Kong brand
  • How the climate crisis generation can usher in an era of responsible capitalism
  • Shanghai lockdown: Covid cases hit new low as city unveils 50-point exit strategy with subsidies, spending coupons to repair economy
  • Hong Kong investors stung by stock market losses seek refuge in low-risk products, brokers say
  • How to age well: tennis, dancing, massage and meditation keep Malaysian grandma happy and active at 78
  • History of real pirates who dressed as passengers to hijack and plunder Hong Kong ships in the South China Sea
  • Rihanna, Angelababy and Zhang Ziyi wear his fabulous dresses: fashion designer Giambattista Valli on new Mytheresa collection and Asian clients who ‘embrace beauty’
  • From M+ and Palace museums to Ocean Park’s Water World, five Hong Kong attractions, new or updated, to wow tourists
  • How Ringo Lam crime thriller Full Alert mixed realistic action on Hong Kong streets with premonition of 1997 handover
  • Post-pandemic dating tips to keep in mind – treat it like a job interview, dress for the occasion and try to have fun meeting a new person
  • How cutting-edge ‘robotic’ prosthetics developed for amputees could transform the able-bodied into augmented cyborgs
  • Vignettes from a side of China we don’t often see in Chinese-Canadian author’s short story collection Taobao
  • Art Basel 2022: how digital innovation saved Hong Kong’s art market – virtual exhibitions, online masterclasses and live-streamed studio visits helped the ‘glocal’ scene get ‘phygital’
  • Liam Payne’s rebound? Meet Aliana Mawla, the model dating the One Direction star just a month after his break-up with ex fiancée Maya Henry … but they first met in 2018 on a music video
  • Where is Priscilla Presley today? Elvis Presley’s ex-wife just opened up about her controversial marriage, sold her US$13 million Beverly Hills luxury mansion and praised Baz Luhrmann’s new biopic
  • Who is Gabriel Aubry, Charlize Theron’s rumoured new model boyfriend? He walked for Tommy Hilfiger, Calvin Klein and Ralph Lauren, has a child with Halle Berry and dated Kim Kardashian
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

Wired is a full-color monthly American magazine, published in both print and online editions, that reports on how emerging technologies affect culture, the economy and politics. Monthly edition, best run at the start of every month.

  • Qubit teleportation between non-neighbouring nodes in a quantum network
  • Realizing repeated quantum error correction in a distance-three surface code
  • Demonstration of fault-tolerant universal quantum gate operations
  • Cilia metasurfaces for electronically programmable microfluidic manipulation
  • Cobalt-electrocatalytic HAT for functionalization of unsaturated C–C bonds
  • Enhanced silica export in a future ocean triggers global diatom decline
  • Hmx gene conservation identifies the origin of vertebrate cranial ganglia
  • Mosquito brains encode unique features of human odour to drive host seeking
  • Emergent reliability in sensory cortical coding and inter-area communication
  • Transcriptomic mapping uncovers Purkinje neuron plasticity driving learning
  • Extricating human tumour immune alterations from tissue inflammation
  • Intracellular lipid surveillance by small G protein geranylgeranylation
  • Intermittent PI3Kδ inhibition sustains anti-tumour immunity and curbs irAEs
  • PHGDH heterogeneity potentiates cancer cell dissemination and metastasis
  • Transcriptional coupling of distant regulatory genes in living embryos
  • Maternal inheritance of glucose intolerance via oocyte TET3 insufficiency
  • Molecular basis for the initiation of DNA primer synthesis
  • Author Correction: Fluctuating interaction network and time-varying stability of a natural fish community
  • Author Correction: The loss of ions from Venus through the plasma wake
  • Publisher Correction: Reproducible brain-wide association studies require thousands of individuals
Nature is a weekly international multidisciplinary scientific journal publishing peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nauture also provides rapid, authoritative, insightful and arresting news and interpretation of topical and coming trends affecting science, scientists and the wider public.

Popular Science. Monthly magazine. Should be downloaded around the middle of each month.

Literary review publishing essay-length book reviews and topical articles on politics, literature, history, philosophy, science and the arts by leading writers and thinkers

entry.title: Asian Review of Books: 27 May, 2022
entry.summary: Asian Review of Books: 27 May, 2022 published at 2022-05-27 00:36AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • “Solo Dance” by Li Kotomi
  • Podcast with Kerry Brown, co-author of “China Through European Eyes: 800 Years of Cultural and Intellectual Encounter”
  • New Book Announcement: “Into the Desert” by Xuemo
  • Here be Dragons: Asian myths and fables inspiring recent Young Adult Literature
  • “Ogata Kōrin: Art in Early Modern Japan” by Frank Feltens
  • New Book Announcement: “Beethoven in Beijing: Stories from the Philadelphia Orchestra’s Historic Journey to China” by Jennifer Lin
  • “Silent Parade” by Keigo Higashino
  • “Chariot of the Sun: An Informal History of a Siamese Family” by Shane Bunnag
  • “The Bangalore Detectives Club” by Harini Nagendra
  • Podcast with Glynne Walley, translator of “Eight Dogs, or ‘Hakkenden’: Part One—An Ill-Considered Jest” by Kyokutei Bakin
In addition to reviewing books about or of relevance to Asia, the Asian Review of Books also features long-format essays by leading Asian writers and thinkers, to providing an unparalleled forum for discussion of key contemporary issues by Asians for Asia and a vehicle of intellectual depth and breadth where leading thinkers can write on the books, arts and ideas of the day. Widely quoted and referenced, with an archive of more than one thousand book reviews, it is the only web resource dedicated to Asian books. And now, with the addition of the new premium content, the Asian Review of Books, is a must-read publication.
